Vince: Did you feel that? Like someone’s watching us from just out of sight.

Lena: Don’t say that… I felt it too, but I thought it was just my nerves.

Vince: The streets are too quiet. It feels wrong.

Lena: Yeah… like the silence is listening back.

Vince: I swear I keep seeing movement when I turn around.

Lena: Same. But when I look, there’s nothing there.

Vince: We’re being watched, Lena. I’m sure of it.

Lena: I don’t like how sure you sound… because I believe you.

Vince: It started when we entered this district.

Lena: Or when something in this district noticed us.

Vince: Should we go back?

Lena: I don’t think ‘back’ is safe either anymore.

Vince: Every window feels like an eye right now.

Lena: Stop… now I’m seeing it too.

Vince: Whatever this is, it knows we know.

Lena: And I think that made it worse.
